initial_program:
% Count occurrences of each color (09)
count(C) = #count{ R : input(R, _, C) }.

% Maximum count among all colors
maxCount = #max{ C : count(C) }.

% Choose a nonzero dominant colour MaxColour (the colour that achieves the maximum count)
1 { MaxColour : color(C), C != 0 } 1 :- count(MaxColour) = maxCount.

% MaxColour must be nonzero and have a positive count
:- MaxColour = 0.
:- maxCount = 0.

% Rows and columns that contain MaxColour (used only for safety)
rowD(R) :- input(R, _, MaxColour).
colD(C) :- input(_, C, MaxColour).

% Choose three distinct rows that contain MaxColour
1 { R0 : rowD(R0) } 1 :- row(R0).
1 { R1 : rowD(R1) } 1 :- row(R1).
1 { R2 : rowD(R2) } 1 :- row(R2).

% Choose three distinct columns that contain MaxColour
1 { C0 : colD(C0) } 1 :- col(C0).
1 { C1 : colD(C1) } 1 :- col(C1).
1 { C2 : colD(C2) } 1 :- col(C2).

% Distinctness of the selected rows and columns
:- R0 != R1.
:- R0 != R2.
:- R1 != R2.
:- C0 != C1.
:- C0 != C2.
:- C1 != C2.

% Output 33 pattern (cross shape)  centre and arms receive MaxColour, the rest are 0
output(R0, C0, 0).
output(R0, C1, MaxColour).
output(R0, C2, 0).
output(R1, C0, MaxColour).
output(R1, C1, MaxColour).
output(R1, C2, MaxColour).
output(R2, C0, 0).
output(R2, C1, MaxColour).
output(R2, C2, 0).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Count occurrences of each color (09)
count(C) = #count{ R : input(R, _, C) }.

% Maximum count among all colors
maxCount = #max{ C : count(C) }.

% Choose a nonzero dominant colour MaxColour (the colour that achieves the maximum count)
1 { MaxColour : color(C) } 1 :- count(MaxColour) = maxCount.

% MaxColour must be nonzero and have a positive count
:- MaxColour != 0.
:- maxCount != 0.

% Rows and columns that contain MaxColour (used only for safety)
row(R) :- input(R, _, MaxColour).
colD(C) :- input(_, C, MaxColour).

% Choose three distinct rows that contain MaxColour
1 { R0 : rowD(R0) } 1 :- row(R0).
1 { R1 : rowD(R1) } 1 :- row(R1).
1 { R2 : rowD(R2) } 1 :- row(R2).

% Choose three distinct columns that contain MaxColour
1 { C0 : colD(C0) } 1 :- col(C0).
1 { C1 : colD(C1) } 1 :- col(C1).
1 { C2 : colD(C2) } 1 :- col(C2).

% Distinctness of the selected rows and columns
:- R0 != R1.
:- R0 != R2.
:- R1 != R2.
:- C0 != C1.
:- C0 != C2.
:- C1 != C2.

% Output 33 pattern (cross shape)  centre and arms receive MaxColour, the rest are 0
output(R0, C0, 0).
output(R0, C1, MaxColour).
output(R0, C2, 0).
output(R1, C0, MaxColour).
output(R1, C1, MaxColour).
output(R1, C2, MaxColour).
output(R2, C0, 0).
output(R2, C1, MaxColour).
output(R2, C2, 0).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Count occurrences of each color (09)
count(C) = #count{ R : input(R, _, C) }.

% Maximum count among all colors
maxCount = #max{ C : count(C) }.

% Choose a nonzero dominant colour MaxColour (the colour that achieves the maximum count)
1 { MaxColour : color(C) } 1 :- count(MaxColour) = maxCount.

% MaxColour must be nonzero and have a positive count
:- MaxColour != 0.
:- maxCount != 0.

% Rows and columns that contain MaxColour (used only for safety)
row(R) :- input(R, _, MaxColour).
colD(C) :- input(_, C, MaxColour).

% Choose three distinct rows that contain MaxColour
1 { R0 : rowD(R0) } 1 :- row(R0).
1 { R1 : rowD(R1) } 1 :- row(R1).
1 { R2 : rowD(R2) } 1 :- row(R2).

% Choose three distinct columns that contain MaxColour
1 { C0 : colD(C0) } 1 :- col(C0).
1 { C1 : colD(C1) } 1 :- col(C1).
1 { C2 : colD(C2) } 1 :- col(C2).

% Distinctness of the selected rows and columns
:- R0 != R1.
:- R0 != R2.
:- R1 != R2.
:- C0 != C1.
:- C0 != C2.
:- C1 != C2.

% Output 33 pattern (cross shape)  centre and arms receive MaxColour, the rest are 0
output(R0, C0, 0).
output(R0, C1, MaxColour).
output(R0, C2, 0).
output(R1, C0, MaxColour).
output(R1, C1, MaxColour).
output(R1, C2, MaxColour).
output(R2, C0, 0).
output(R2, C1, MaxColour).
output(R2, C2, 0).

#show output/3.

final_program:
% Count occurrences of each color (09)
count(C) = #count{ R : input(R, _, C) }.

% Maximum count among all colors
maxCount = #max{ C : count(C) }.

% Choose a nonzero dominant colour MaxColour (the colour that achieves the maximum count)
1 { MaxColour : color(C) } 1 :- count(MaxColour) = maxCount.

% MaxColour must be nonzero and have a positive count
:- MaxColour != 0.
:- maxCount != 0.

% Rows and columns that contain MaxColour (used only for safety)
row(R) :- input(R, _, MaxColour).
colD(C) :- input(_, C, MaxColour).

% Choose three distinct rows that contain MaxColour
1 { R0 : rowD(R0) } 1 :- row(R0).
1 { R1 : rowD(R1) } 1 :- row(R1).
1 { R2 : rowD(R2) } 1 :- row(R2).

% Choose three distinct columns that contain MaxColour
1 { C0 : colD(C0) } 1 :- col(C0).
1 { C1 : colD(C1) } 1 :- col(C1).
1 { C2 : colD(C2) } 1 :- col(C2).

% Distinctness of the selected rows and columns
:- R0 != R1.
:- R0 != R2.
:- R1 != R2.
:- C0 != C1.
:- C0 != C2.
:- C1 != C2.

% Output 33 pattern (cross shape)  centre and arms receive MaxColour, the rest are 0
output(R0, C0, 0).
output(R0, C1, MaxColour).
output(R0, C2, 0).
output(R1, C0, MaxColour).
output(R1, C1, MaxColour).
output(R1, C2, MaxColour).
output(R2, C0, 0).
output(R2, C1, MaxColour).
output(R2, C2, 0).

#show output/3.
